methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $31M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $125M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: NODA AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $125M

data.narrative:
CX2 Industries is a Series A defense and security company that has raised $31M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 peers—2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms in the same sector and stage cohort, including Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Code Metal, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns, among others. The private peer set ranges from $125M to $3.0B in valuation.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $31M raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to later-stage advancement, yielding $62M. Peer-median analysis across the 8 comparable private companies yields a median valuation of $1.0B, with a range spanning $125M to $3.0B. These two methods produce divergent signals, reflecting the wide dispersion in defense-tech valuations at this stage.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $53M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $531M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around whether CX2 will command a multiple closer to earlier-stage, capital-constrained benchmarks or closer to the elevated valuations of later-stage defense peers. Stage progression alone suggests the lower end; peer comparables suggest substantially higher valuations are defensible in this sector. Specific product adoption, competitive positioning, and the timing of the next funding round will be material to narrowing this range.
